title: Positional issue title and type flag
description:
Make the natural first guess for creating and typing an issue work: a positional title and a long-only type flag.

## Background

`issue create` currently accepts only `--title` (cli.rs:436-438), so `jit issue create "Title"` with the title positional fails. There is no `--type` flag, and `-t` is already bound to `--title`, so a type flag must be long-only. Type validity already flows through the config `[type_hierarchy]` (config.toml:24) and the baked schema (validation/defaults.rs:192), so no type list belongs in the CLI.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: jit issue create "Title" succeeds with the title taken positionally, and the --title flag form remains accepted.
- [hard] REQ-02: issue create and issue update accept --type <kind>, write a type:<kind> label, and reject a kind not declared in config through the existing validation layer.

## Notes

Touch cli.rs and the create/update handlers; `--type` writes a `type:<kind>` label validated through the existing validation layer (config.toml:24, validation/defaults.rs:192). Update doc-comments in the same change.
